placement_rationale: This file lives in 06_LEARNING_LAYER/SIGNAL_WEIGHT_CALIBRATION/signal_weights/ (NOT shadow/). It is a machine-readable view of LL4_PREDICTION_PRIOR_v1_0.md §4-§5 qualitative findings — a recommendation/diagnostic artifact, not a weight register subject to SHADOW_MODE_PROTOCOL §3 promotion rules. Per SHADOW_MODE_PROTOCOL §2 LL.4 row, LL.4 produces no shadow→production split at M4. The file is consumed as a prior recommendation by future M5 LL.5 prior-fitting work and by retrieval-time fallback weighting for signals not in the LL.1 promotion set; it does NOT carry per-edge or per-signal calibrated weights and does NOT govern the LL.1 production register.

consumer_contract.fallback_role: Use as prior recommendation for signals NOT in the LL.1 promotion set, and as a retrieval-time hint for date-precision modulation. Once M5 LL.5 fits calibrated multipliers from prediction_ledger.jsonl outcomes, those calibrated multipliers supersede these qualitative tiers.

global_modifier.name: date_precision
global_modifier.rule: Modulate the basis-class and domain-class priors below by the date precision of the retrieval target.

tiers.source: held_out partition mean lit_score for temporal_engine basis (n=229 expected_observations)
tiers.caveat: Held-out figure is contaminated by selection bias toward precise dates per LL4 §2.2 H1; treat as upper bound under the contaminated regime.

tiers.definition: Year + month known; day unknown or approximate
tiers.source: training partition mean lit_score for temporal_engine basis (n=863 expected_observations)
tiers.caveat: Training figure is the more honest baseline (no held-out selection bias) but still inherits LEL retrodictive_match labeling bias per LL4 §2.2 H2.

tiers.source: approx-month estimate × ~0.85 reduction (informal; no fit at n=37)
tiers.caveat: Coefficient is informal — use only as a directional hint, not a calibrated multiplier.

domain_priors.rationale: Highest-n observed domain bucket. Lit-rate 0.50 in training; apply ~0.5-multiplier on temporal_engine retrievals from this domain. Modulate by global date_precision.

Worst-performing observed domain at 0.30 lit-rate. The under-performance is design — natal-permanent yoga signals (Pancha Mahapurusha cluster + ancillaries) fire only on a subset of events. Apply ~0.30-multiplier on general-bucket temporal retrievals AND consult LL3_DOMAIN_COHERENCE §5.1 R.LL3.2 for cluster-aware consolidation (treat 6-yoga cluster as one consolidated configuration rather than 6 independent firings).
Mid-n observed domain. Lit-rate 0.41 in training; apply ~0.4-multiplier on temporal_engine retrievals. Modulate by global date_precision.
Mid-n observed domain. Lit-rate 0.49 in training; apply ~0.5-multiplier on temporal_engine retrievals. Modulate by global date_precision.
Mid-n observed domain. Lit-rate 0.46 in training; apply ~0.45-multiplier on temporal_engine retrievals. Modulate by global date_precision.
Low-n observed domain (only 5 expected observations across 37 training events). Apply ~0.4-multiplier provisionally; re-tune after M5 LEL expansion improves coverage.

Apparent 1.00 lit-rate is a sample-size artifact at n=7. Do not weight retrievals by this figure. Use unweighted MSR routing per LL3_DOMAIN_COHERENCE §5.1 R.LL3.3 with n=7 disclaimer attached at consumption time.

Apparent 1.00 lit-rate is a sample-size artifact at n=6. Do not weight retrievals by this figure. The bucket itself is large in MSR (94 signals) but heavily under-indexed by the rubric to date — see LL3_DOMAIN_COHERENCE §2 unobserved-bucket finding.

Education is structurally absent from MSR's domain ontology (msr_domain_buckets.json); n=3 reflects manual annotation of career-bucket signals as education-flavored in match_notes, not formal MSR domain assignment. Treat as advisory.
Apparent 1.00 lit-rate is a sample-size artifact at n=1. Do not weight retrievals by this figure. Use unweighted MSR routing per LL3_DOMAIN_COHERENCE §5.1 R.LL3.3 with n=1 disclaimer attached.

signal_class_priors.rationale: Perfect calibration in both partitions. When the rubric expects a classical-rule signal to fire, it fires. Apply full prior-weight credit to retrievals on classical-rule basis signals (most directly: natal-permanent yoga retrievals).
Perfect calibration in both partitions. 'both' = signal supported by classical rule AND temporal-engine evaluation. Apply full prior-weight credit.

Temporal_engine signals carry the timing-error variance — they depend on date precision and the dasha/transit/yogini engine's correctness. Variance is interpreted as a date-precision artifact more than a model artifact (consistent with M3-D 10/10 CONSISTENT held-out sample at exact dates). Apply lit-rate weighted credit modulated by the global date_precision tier above.

n1_disclaimer: All findings derive from a single native (n=1) corpus of 37 training events plus a 9-event held-out validity set. Recommendations are diagnostic priors, not statistically significant findings. Per MACRO_PLAN §3.5.A Principle 1. The held_out=0.913 figure must NOT be quoted as a clean validity number — it is contaminated by the held-out selection bias documented in LL4_PREDICTION_PRIOR §2.2 Hypothesis 1.
post_nap_m45_note: This priors file was authored at M4-B-S5-NAP-M45-EXECUTE — same session that closed NAP.M4.5 with 30/30 LL.1 signals approved to production. The basis-class statistics in §3 of the LL.4 source document do NOT need refresh from the pass_2 demotions side (no demotions occurred). The priors above are unchanged from the LL4 v1.0 source document; this file is a machine-readable view of those qualitative findings, not a re-derivation.
